channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AI coding assistants limited but helpful, developers say
item.description: AI coding assistants still need to mature, but they are already helpful according to several attendees of a recent Silicon Valley event for software developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI Rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 Billion Takeover Bid - San Francisco News
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO, CA - OpenAI board rejects billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk's bid to buy the company for $97.4 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Local history buffs are turning to Facebook to share memories of bygone eras
item.description: Social media has a reputation for capturing ephemeral thoughts and images, but around the world, people are using Facebook for a different purpose, setting up groups to record and share images and memories of the past.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Worrying YouTube security flaw exposed billions of user emails
item.description: Experts find a simple flaw could have exposed any email attached to a YouTube account until recently.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Moss Landing battery fire: Residents sue Vistra, others for damages
item.description: A group of Monterey County residents filed a lawsuit in Alameda County against several companies for damages following the large fire at the Moss Landing Battery Plant on Jan. 16.
item.dc:creator: Kyarra Harris, The Mercury News

item.title: Prime Video’s Legally Blonde series has found its Elle Woods, and I’m delighted for the comeback of one of the best ‘00s movies
item.description: Prime Video has finally found its young Elle Woods for its Legally Blonde prequel series – I just hope we get a Jennifer Coolidge cameo.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Apple’s emotional lamp and the future of robots
item.description: Pixar Animation Studios has an unusual logo. The basic logo is the word “Pixar.” But sometimes, an animated lamp named Luxo Jr. hops into the frame and jumps on the letter “i.” The lap exudes personality and represents Pixar’s ability to turn any object into a compelling character.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 tips for being a more decisive leader
item.description: When you lead a team, your people will turn to you to make the final call on a huge number of decisions. People admire a leader who is decisive. But decisiveness is challenging to master. You have to live with the decisions you make.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck owner says he’s been threatened and harassed soon after Musk endorsed Trump
item.description: Dr. Kumait Jaroje said someone put a profane sticker on his gold Cybertruck. When he posted a photo of it on social media he told police he started getting threats online.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Why asteroid 2024 YR4 is unlikely to hit Earth in 2032 and how scientists keep track
item.description: Scientists say the threat of a newly discovered asteroid has risen slightly in the past few weeks, as the world's telescopes rush to track its course.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rethinking the enterprise network backbone
item.description: Multiple business imperatives are driving CIOs to re-examine and re-invent their approach to network infrastructure, including the mission critical backbone that supports highly complex, bandwidth-intensive, multi-cloud environments. One emerging alternative: backbone-as-a-service (BBaaS) offerings.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why your IoT devices are the weakest link in security
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Svetlin Todorov, Fast Company

item.title: CISPE raises red alert over Broadcom’s VMware licensing changes
item.description: The European Cloud Competition Observatory (ECCO), a monitoring body established by the association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) as a way to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region, this week came down hard on Broadcom for its failure to do so.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tech security 101: How to spot a hidden tracker on your car
item.description: 80% of stalking victims are tracked using tech. GPS trackers, like AirTags, are among the most popular ways to do it. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI company Ross Intelligence loses copyright fight with Thomson Reuters
item.description: A US judge has ruled in favor of Thomson Reuters in a AI training fight against Ross Intelligence, a legal AI startup, according to The Verge. Thomson Reuters sued Ross Intelligence in 2020 for using the company’s legal research platform Westlaw to train Ross Intelligence’s AI without permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rust memory management explained
item.description: The Rust programming language shares many concepts with other languages intended for systems programming. For instance, Rust distinguishes between memory allocated from the stack and memory allocated from the heap.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don’t use public ASP.NET keys (duh), Microsoft warns
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence in December observed a “threat actor” using a publicly available ASP.NET machine key to inject malicious code and fetch the Godzilla post-exploitation framework, a “backdoor” web shell used by intruders to execute commands and manipulate files.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top translation apps for travelers
item.description: Travel abroad worry-free with these top translation apps for travelers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson highlights some favorites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
